title: Black Friday Madness at the Mall

all.en: The alarm rang at four in the morning, but Wei-ting was already wide awake with excitement.
all.zh: 鬧鐘在凌晨四點響起，但偉廷早已興奮得睡不著。

all.en: Today was Black Friday, the busiest shopping day of the year.
all.zh: 今天是黑色星期五，是一年中最忙碌的購物日。

all.en: She grabbed her coat, her wallet, and a list of items she desperately wanted.
all.zh: 她抓起外套、錢包，還有一張寫著她極想購買的清單。

all.en: By the time she arrived, the line outside the department store stretched around the block.
all.zh: 當她抵達時，百貨公司外的隊伍已經繞了整個街區。

all.en: When the doors finally opened, the crowd surged forward like a wave.
all.zh: 當大門終於打開時，人群像浪潮般湧入。

all.en: She managed to grab a flat-screen television that was marked down by seventy percent.
all.zh: 她成功搶到了一台打了七折的平面電視。

all.en: However, two shoppers were already fighting over the last gaming console on the shelf.
all.zh: 然而，已經有兩位顧客正在搶最後一台遊戲主機。

all.en: The cashier looked exhausted as she scanned hundreds of items per hour.
all.zh: 收銀員一邊每小時掃描上百件商品，一邊顯得疲憊不堪。

all.en: Wei-ting saved nearly fifteen thousand dollars in total, which felt like a personal victory.
all.zh: 偉廷總共省下了將近一萬五千元，感覺像是一場個人的勝利。

all.en: On the way home, she realized she had bought a blender she didn't really need.
all.zh: 在回家的路上，她意識到自己買了一台根本用不到的果汁機。

all.en: Still, the thrill of hunting for bargains made the early wake-up worth every minute.
all.zh: 不過，搶便宜的刺激感讓那麼早起床的每一分鐘都值得了。

all.en: That evening, she shared her victory photos with friends on social media.
all.zh: 那天晚上，她在社群媒體上和朋友分享了戰利品的照片。

all.en: Several friends immediately replied, asking if she could shop for them next year.
all.zh: 幾個朋友立刻回覆，問她明年能不能也幫他們搶貨。

all.en: She unpacked the television and discovered a small crack along the back panel.
all.zh: 她拆開電視盒，發現背板沿線有一道小小的裂痕。

all.en: Returning a Black Friday item turned out to be much harder than buying one.
all.zh: 退掉黑色星期五的商品，結果比購買時還要困難得多。

all.en: The customer service line on Saturday morning lasted more than two whole hours.
all.zh: 週六早上的客服排隊時間竟然超過了整整兩個小時。

all.en: Wei-ting promised herself she would research products carefully before the next big sale.
all.zh: 偉廷向自己保證，下一次大特價前一定會先仔細研究商品。

all.en: She listed her unused blender online and sold it within just two days.
all.zh: 她把沒用過的果汁機上架到網路上，兩天內就賣出去了。

all.en: Her younger brother teased her for being so dramatic about saving money.
all.zh: 她弟弟取笑她，省錢省得也太誇張了。

all.en: Still, she calculated that her overall savings outweighed the broken television problem.
all.zh: 不過，她算了一下，整體省下的金額還是多過電視故障的損失。

all.en: Next year, she plans to shop only online to avoid the chaotic crowds.
all.zh: 明年，她打算只在網路上購物，避開混亂的人群。

all.en: She organized her receipts neatly and tracked every discounted item inside a small notebook.
all.zh: 她把收據整齊收好，並在一本小筆記本裡記錄每一件折扣商品。

all.en: Her friends were impressed by how methodically she handled the holiday shopping pressure.
all.zh: 她的朋友們很佩服她應付假日購物壓力的條理分明。

all.en: She decided to donate several barely-used items to a local charity before the new year.
all.zh: 她決定在新年前，把幾件幾乎沒用過的物品捐給當地慈善機構。

all.en: Her mother praised her thoughtful balance between saving money and helping people in need.
all.zh: 她母親稱讚她在省錢和幫助有需要的人之間，取得了體貼的平衡。

all.en: She also began researching honest review websites to avoid overpriced fake bargains.
all.zh: 她也開始研究誠實的評論網站，以免買到價格虛抬的假特價商品。

all.en: One website warned her about a popular brand known for misleading discount labels.
all.zh: 有個網站警告她，某個熱門品牌因為誤導性的折扣標籤而出名。

all.en: She shared the warning on social media, and many strangers thanked her for the tip.
all.zh: 她在社群上分享這個提醒，許多陌生人都感謝她的這個小撇步。

all.en: By December, she had finished her holiday gift list completely within her planned budget.
all.zh: 到了十二月，她已在預定預算內完整買齊所有的節日禮物。

all.en: Her family enjoyed a calm, joyful gathering instead of stressing about overspent credit cards.
all.zh: 她的家人享受了平靜歡樂的聚會，而不是為刷爆的信用卡而焦慮。

all.en: Black Friday had taught her that smart planning matters more than the loudest deal sign.
all.zh: 黑色星期五教會她，聰明的規劃比最響亮的特價招牌更重要。

all.en: The next year, she returned to the mall a full week before Black Friday.
all.zh: 隔年，她在黑色星期五整整一週前就先到了購物中心。

all.en: She quietly compared regular prices and saved photos as honest reference for her records.
all.zh: 她安靜地比較平日價格，把照片存下來作為自己誠實的參考紀錄。

all.en: When the big day came, she easily spotted fake discounts hidden behind flashy signs.
all.zh: 大日子來臨時，她輕易就辨認出藏在花俏招牌後面的假折扣。

all.en: Her teenage daughter joined her, eager to learn the same calm shopping strategy.
all.zh: 她青春期的女兒陪著她，渴望學會這套同樣冷靜的購物策略。

all.en: Together they walked past loud crowds and laughed at the silly fighting over towels.
all.zh: 她們一起走過喧鬧的人群，對著為毛巾爭搶的滑稽場面笑了出來。

all.en: Her daughter bought one quality coat that would last several winters without regret.
all.zh: 女兒買了一件高品質大衣，可以毫無後悔地穿過好幾個冬天。

all.en: They ended the day with hot chocolate at a quiet cafe far from the chaos.
all.zh: 她們在遠離混亂的安靜咖啡店裡，以熱可可結束了這一天。

all.en: At home, she taught her daughter how to track future prices using a free app.
all.zh: 回到家，她教女兒如何用一款免費的應用程式追蹤未來的價格。

all.en: Her daughter later shared the app with classmates struggling with their first part-time wages.
all.zh: 女兒後來把那款應用程式分享給為第一份打工薪水所苦的同學。

all.en: Black Friday had become a family lesson in patience, planning, and quiet financial wisdom.
all.zh: 黑色星期五已成為這個家族關於耐心、規劃與低調理財智慧的一堂課。
